titleStmt.title: Letter from Bishop Patrick McKenna to John Francis Hogan, 11 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick McKenna

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was addressed to Rev Dr John Francis Hogan (1858–1918), a priest, author, and president of St Patrick's College, Maynooth.The letter was sent by Roman Catholic Bishop, Patrick McKenna (1868-1942). In this letter Mckenna is informing Hogan of the holidays he is granting to 'P.C.' who is suffering from erythema, a skin condition. He must stay indoors while his illness lasts and thus must be granted a holiday.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
5/11 BISHOPS HOUSE,
MONAGHAN 11th Jan 1916. My Dear Mons. Hogan. I beg to inform you
that I have granted an extension
of holidays to Mgr. P. C. Dynan
as he is suffering from Erythema
of the face and has been ordered
by the Doctor to keep within doors
while the swelling lasts. I have extended his holidays to Thursday
20th & if he is not fit to travel
then, I am directing him to apply
again. Kindest regards,
I am, my dear Mons. Hogan,
Faithfully yous,
+ Patrick McKenna
